shared_properties: Player body scale stays 16x24 px per frame and keeps the chained-prisoner silhouette visible against dark walls.
All clips preserve a centered torso pivot so downward camera tracking feels stable across fall, friction, and hit states.
differences: This atlas combines fall_loop, friction_left, friction_right, and hit_flash states in one deterministic ten-frame sheet.

reference_images.label: Slip Down player concept
reference_images.purpose: Prisoner body proportion, chain placement, and fall pose grounding.

reference_images.label: Slip Down moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Shadow contrast and dungeon lighting grounding.

reference_images.label: Slip Down GDD mirror
reference_images.purpose: Frame counts, sprite size, hit effect timing, and friction pose grounding.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Use TextureAtlasLayout with a PlayerVisualState component that swaps clips by movement and damage state.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load character/prisoner_descent_state_sheet.png through AssetServer and pair it with a clip table keyed by asset_key.
runtime_notes: Fall loop uses four frames at 8 FPS.
Friction clips and hit clip keep the feet aligned to the same baseline for collision readability.

context.role: Primary playable character art used during all descent, braking, and damage states.
context.usage: Visible whenever the player is active in the shaft and during the clear-room arrival state.

deliverables.notes: Ten-frame atlas arranged as five columns by two rows.
Clip map for fall_loop, friction_left, friction_right, and hit_flash states.

acceptance_criteria: Sheet size is exactly 80x48 px with ten 16x24 frames.
Fall loop uses four frames, friction_left uses two frames, friction_right uses two frames, and hit_flash uses two frames with the declared clip ranges.
All frames keep the body centered so camera-follow jitter does not appear when clips switch inside FixedUpdate.
